observations.managementAction: On-going    reconciliati on of records. Gathering of necessary documents to support the request for write off.
The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer committed to address the matter, and to make the necessary adjustments. The reconciliation is still on-going.

The balance of the PPE account per books was not reconciled with the balance per RPCPPE as of December 31, 2020. Audit recommendations is highly appreciated. We will take necessary actions to reconcile the said differences.
Physical count of PPEs already done. We are still gathering sufficient data to support the disposal of unserviceable PPEs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant  submit  the  journal  entry
   vouchers to recognize any unissued items from the construction  materials
   amounting to P341,573.35 including the proofs of  issuances  thereof.  In
   case all had been issued, the proofs of completion of  the  repairs  done
   must also be submitted for verification.  This  is  to  ensure  that  the
   affected accounts be presented fairly in the books of the LGU.

We recommended that Management:
   a. initiate an inquiry  with  the  identified  account  with  regard  the
      balances  in  the  books  of  the  Source  Agencies  and   request   a
      certification thereof duly signed by the  Agency  Accountant  and  the
      Head of the Agency;
   b. direct the Municipal Accountant to review the status of  the  accounts
      and observe the guidelines and procedures on the write-off of  Dormant
      Payable accounts as prescribed in COA Circular 2016-005 dated December
      19, 2016;
   c. instruct the Municipal Accountant to conduct  a  thorough  assessment,
      evaluation and investigation of the outstanding  accounts  and  record
      adjustments thereof; and
   d. direct the Municipal Accountant  to  make  full  disclosure  of  facts
      pertinent to the grant of the KKK loan such  as  the  purpose  of  the
      loan, date granted, amount utilized/liquidation.

We  reiterated  our  recommendation  that  the  inventory  and  disposal
   committees conduct inspection and appraisal  of  all  the  unserviceable
   properties of the LGU. Thereafter, the Inventory and  Inspection  Report
   of Unserviceable  Properties  (IIRUP)  be  prepared  as  basis  for  the
   disposal and the dropping of the costs of the disposed  properties  from
   the books of the LGU, thus, present fairly the PPE and Government Equity
   accounts in the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile  his  records  of
   PPE with the physical inventory report and take up all necessary
   adjustments, thus, present the correct balances of the PPE  accounts  in
   the books of the LGU.  In addition,  the  Management  must  require  the
   Supply Officer and Municipal Accountant to maintain the Property,  Plant
   and Equipment Ledger Cards and record the acquisition, description,
   custody, disposal and other information relative to the assets.


   The Supply Officer shall likewise  religiously  maintain  the  inventory
   count report and reconcile the same with the accounting records  of  the
   Municipal Accountant on a regular basis. The  concerned  officers  shall
   investigate  and  evaluate  the  differences,  adjust  the  records   as
   appropriate and submit a revised RPCPPE  based  on  the  reconciliations
   made.

observations.managementAction: Already   submitted the Copy of Official Receipt including the JEV in order to reclassify the entry made for the mentioned amount. Already tagged as complied and closed
Documents   submitted. Explanatory documents submitted.
Evidence of  Services rendered already submitted to the Office of the Auditor for verification.
The Management submitted the corrected BAC Resolution, the list of the intended beneficiaries, the content of the hygiene kit, pictures of the hygiene kits, and the distribution lists per barangay.
observations.reason: The estimated no. of beneficiaries was per barangay, however, it failed to indicate the beneficiaries as to how many for men, women, children, & elementary students were benefitted; The distribution lists totaled only 2,257; and the content did not indicate the size & type, hence, no basis for the prices.

observations.managementAction: The Management justified that the other supplier-quo ters no longer wanted to deliver due to the distance of Maconacon.  In fact, it was recommended by the PDRRMO.  Only the consulting corporation was willing to deliver.  Only the consulting corporation was willing to deliver.  Only the consulting corporation was willing to deliver.
observations.reason: The circumstances must have been duly resolved by the BAC. Management must submit proof that the other supplier-quoters backed out or negated the delivery. Also, the cash advance not included in the contract must be justified/explained. The submitted additional documents will have yet to be verified.

observations.managementAction: The balance of the PPE account per books still did not reconcile with the balance per RPCPPE as of December 31, 2020. Audit recommendations is highly appreciated. We will take necessary actions to reconcile the said differences.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management revisit its staffing pattern. In this regard, the LGU may consider filling in some of its vacant plantilla positions and reduce the number of job order workers, thus, facilitate monitoring of workers and establish more accountability at work.
observations.reason: Hence, reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Management consider the charging of a minimal mark-up or profit to shoulder the expenses and/or possible losses in the handling and safekeeping of the goods prior to sale. Also, Management establish an appropriate stock level of rice and other agricultural supplies to avoid under/over stocking.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal  Agriculturist
   reconcile their records on the breeding stocks of  the  LGU.  Thereafter,
   the Municipal Accountant take up the necessary  adjustments  with  proper
   disclosures to present  fairly  the  balance/s  of  the  account  in  the
   financial statements.

We recommended that Management  observe  the  proper  guidelines  in  the
   inventory and disposal of the unserviceable/destroyed properties  of  the
   LGU.  Correspondingly,   the   Inventory   and   Inspection   Report   of
   Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) be prepared as basis  for  the  disposal
   and the possible dropping of the cost of the disposed properties  in  the
   books of the LGU, thus, present fairly  the  PPE  and  Government  Equity
   accounts in the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant take up funds granted to the
   barangays and the school for specified purposes as “Due from LGUs and Due
   from NGAs”, respectively, for fair presentation of accounts in the  books
   as well as to establish accountability and demand for liquidation.

We recommended that the Technical Working Group (TWG) of  LGU  Maconacon,
   Isabela  conduct  the   post-qualification   following   the   prescribed
   procedures to serve its purpose,  thus,  ensure  that  all  projects  are
   awarded to and undertaken by bidders/contractors which do not only  offer
   the lowest bid but which are also found complying and responsive  to  all
   the pertinent requirements and conditions.

We recommended that Management ensure that all procurements of  the  LGU
   be supported with complete documentation as prescribed. It is emphasized
   that completeness of documentation means not only the submission of  the
   documents but also  the  correctness  and  consistency  of  the  details
   thereof.

   In this regard, the lacking and necessary corrective documents  for  the
   projects-Construction of  Public  Market  and  Road  Concreting  costing
   P2,000,000.00  and  P8,914,422.00,   respectively,  be   submitted   for
   verification. Should the documents be not  submitted  or  found  not  in
   order, the disbursements may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management ensure that all
   procurements of the LGU be  supported  with  complete  documentation  and
   information  to  prove  the  validity,  legality  and  propriety  of  the
   transaction. ‘

   As regards the procurement of rescue equipment costing  P353,998.00,  the
   lacking and necessary  explanatory  documents  should  be  submitted  for
   verification.  Otherwise,  the   corresponding   disbursement   will   be
   disallowed in audit.

We  recommended  that  Management  submit  the  lacking   and   necessary
   explanatory/corrective documents for the procurement of fuel amounting to
   P1,499,780.72 for verification. Should the documents be not submitted  or
   found not in order, the disbursements may be suspended or  disallowed  in
   audit.

We  recommended  that  Management  submit  the  lacking   and   necessary
   corrective/explanatory documents  for  the  reimbursements  amounting  to
   P132,934.18 for verification. Should the documents be  not  submitted  or
   found not in order, the disbursements may be suspended or  disallowed  in
   audit.

We recommended that Management prepare the Local Disaster Risk  Reduction
   and Management Plan (LDRRMP) in accordance with the pertinent  provisions
   of COA Circular  No.  2012-002.  Accordingly,  the  plan  should  include
   information on the  list  of  projects  and  activities  charged  to  the
   unexpended LDRRMF of previous years.

We recommended that Management  refrain  from  paying  meals  and  snacks
   incurred during  the  regular  sessions  of  the  members  of  the  local
   sanggunian  as  these  are  supposedly  covered   by   their   respective
   Representation Allowances (RA).

   As regards the meals and snacks amounting to  P37,380.00,  the  concerned
   members of the local sanggunian should  refund  the  same,  otherwise,  a
   Notice of Disallowance (ND) will be issued.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management look into the condition of the receivable account and prioritize the collection thereof. If warranted, the Municipal Mayor may request for authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off the receivable following the specific guidelines and procedures as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005, thus, present fairly the affected accounts in the books of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Management requested for authority to write off the account and the request was favorably granted. Hence, the account has been derecognized from the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds to present fairly the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile his records of PPE with the physical inventory report, provide depreciation expenses for all depreciable PPE, and take up all necessary adjustments in the books, thus, establish the fair balance of the PPE account at any given time.

We recommended that Management ensure that all procurements of the LGU be supported with complete documentation as prescribed. It is emphasized that completeness of documentation means not only the submission of the documents but also the correctness of the details thereof. In this regard, the lacking and necessary corrective documents for the procurement of Bulldozer amounting to P8,000,000.00 be submitted for verification. Should the documents be not submitted or found not in order, the disbursement may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management ensure that the utilization of the Special Education Fund (SEF) should be in accord with the approved budget. In this regard, Management submit a justification or an explanation for charging the purchase of a printer costing P47,694.00 against SEF to avoid any possible disallowance.

We recommended that Management ensure that all procurements of the LGU be supported with complete documentation as prescribed. It is emphasized that completeness of documentation means not only the submission of the documents but also the completeness and correctness of the details thereof. In this regard, the lacking and necessary explanatory documents for the procurement of fuel amounting to P2,198,586.35 be submitted for verification. Should the documents be not submitted or found not in order, the disbursement may be suspended or disallowed in audit.
observations.reason: Some purchases of fuel in 2019 were still not supported with complete documentation and information. Hence, reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly observe the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the preparation of plan/budget and reporting of LDRRMF. Accordingly, to facilitate effective and timely audit of the fund, the LDRRMO: prepare the LDRRMFIP using the prescribed format; prepare the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the prescribed format (certified correct by the Municipal Accountant); and submit copy of the monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to COA within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Management has submitted the LGU’s monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, though, not within the prescribed period. Also, the LDRRMFIP for 2019 did not include the list of projects, programs, and activities for the unspent DRRM funds in prior years. Hence, reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prepare the LGU’s Annual GAD Plan and Budget using the prescribed format, i.e. to include information on the gender issues or gender mandates, GAD objective, and attributed Programs. Also, the gender issues must be based on the LGU’s sex disaggregated data (SDD).

We recommended that Management ensure that payments of mobilization expenses to contractors be supported with complete documentation as prescribed to prove the validity, legality and propriety thereof. As regards the mobilization expenses amounting to P584,734.03 in 2018, the corresponding letter-requests and the irrevocable letters of credit or bank guarantees from/issued by the latter be submitted for verification. Should the documents be not submitted or found not in order, the disbursements may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management install and maintain a system of internal control for the procurement, recording and monitoring of Supplies and Property, Plant and Equipment, as follows: The GSO should be responsible in accepting procured goods after inspection. Thus, he should not be designated as Inspector or a canvasser.; The procured items be received, accepted and recorded in the Stock or Property Cards by the Supplies/Property Officer. Stock Cards and Property Cards be maintained for each type of Supplies/PPE, respectively.; The Supply/Property Officer issues supplies/small PPE items to the requesting office or end-users. The issuances should be supported with Requisition and Issue Slips. The issuance of small items not to be considered as PPE should be covered with Inventory Custodian Slips (ICS).; The Accounting Office should also maintain complete Supplies Ledger Cards and PPE Ledger Cards for supplies and PPE, respectively.; and The Supply/Property Office and Accounting Office reconcile their respective records regularly.
observations.reason: Deficiencies were still noted in the procurement of PPE in 2019. The deficiencies noted were included in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly observe the pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 in the procurement of infrastructure projects and to support the corresponding disbursements with the prescribed documentary requirements. As regards the procurement of Core Local Access Road costing 13,848,626.12, the lacking documents and the necessary explanation/justification for the deficiencies noted be submitted for verification. Should the documents be not submitted or found not in order, the disbursements may be suspended or disallowed in audit.
observations.reason: Procurements of similar projects in 2019 were still not supported with completed documentation. The audit observations were included in the Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management revisit the allocation of funds for the Pag-ibig contributions (government share) for its employees, i.e. they may consider setting a fixed or maximum amount of additional government share for all the employees regardless of the amount of their increased contributions for purposes of equity and efficiency.

We recommended that Management revisit the grant of financial assistance to other LGUs especially to those belonging to higher income classification considering the financial capacity of the LGU.  If warranted, Management may also make proper representation with the other LGUs in the Province of Isabela for the evaluation of the practice on exchange of financial assistance between and among the LGUs in the province.
We recommended that Management ensure that programs, projects, and activities (PPAs) for Solid Waste Management are feasible and are implemented as programmed to realize/serve the intended purposes.
observations.reason: Though most projects and programs identified for solid waste management were implemented, the sanitary landfill has not yet been established. Hence, included in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management revisit the program on the purchase and storage of rice and agricultural supplies for sale without mark-up to the constituents during rainy days or period of calamity. In this regard, a minimal mark-up or profit may be considered to shoulder the expenses and/or possible losses in the handling and safekeeping of the goods prior to sale.
observations.reason: The disposal of rice and other agricultural supplies in 2018 were still at cost or without mark-up. Per FS, the Sales account has the same balance as the Cost of Sales, thus, zero revenue. Hence, reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that Management look into the  condition
   of the receivable account  and  prioritize  the  collection  thereof.  If
   warranted, the  Municipal  Mayor  may  request  for  authority  from  the
   Commission on Audit to write-off the receivable  following  the  specific
   guidelines and procedures as prescribed under COA Circular No.  2016-005,
   thus, present fairly the affected accounts in the books of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal  Accountant  reconcile  the  reciprocal
   accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds  to  present  fairly
   the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile his records of PPE
   with the physical inventory report, provide depreciation expenses for all
   depreciable PPE, and take up all  necessary  adjustments  in  the  books,
   thus, establish the fair balance of the PPE account at any given time.

We recommended that Management ensure that all procurements of  the  LGU
   be supported with complete documentation as prescribed. It is emphasized
   that completeness of documentation means not only the submission of  the
   documents but also the correctness of the details thereof.

   In this regard, the lacking and necessary corrective documents  for  the
   procurement of Bulldozer amounting to  P8,000,000.00  be  submitted  for
   verification. Should the documents be not  submitted  or  found  not  in
   order, the disbursement may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management ensure that the utilization of the Special
   Education Fund (SEF) should be in accord with  the  approved  budget.  In
   this regard, Management submit a  justification  or  an  explanation  for
   charging the purchase of a printer  costing  P47,694.00  against  SEF  to
   avoid any possible disallowance.

We recommended that Management ensure that all procurements of the LGU be
   supported with complete documentation as  prescribed.  It  is  emphasized
   that completeness of documentation means not only the submission  of the
   documents but also  the  completeness  and  correctness  of  the  details
   thereof.

   In this regard, the lacking and necessary explanatory documents  for  the
   procurement  of  fuel  amounting  to  P2,198,586.35  be   submitted   for
   verification. Should the documents be  not  submitted  or  found  not  in
   order, the disbursement may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the pertinent  provisions
   of COA Circular No.  2012-002  on  the  preparation  of  plan/budget  and
   reporting of LDRRMF. Accordingly,  to  facilitate  effective  and  timely
   audit of the fund, the LDRRMO:

    • prepare the LDRRMFIP using the prescribed format;
    • prepare the monthly Report on the Sources  and  Utilization  of  DRRMF
      using the  prescribed  format  (certified  correct  by  the  Municipal
      Accountant); and
    • submit copy of the monthly Report on the Sources  and  Utilization  of
      DRRMF to COA within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management prepare the  LGU’s  Annual  GAD  Plan  and
   Budget using the prescribed format, i.e. to include  information  on  the
   gender issues or gender mandates, GAD objective, and attributed Programs.
   Also, the gender issues must be based on the LGU’s sex disaggregated data
   (SDD).

We recommended that  Management  ensure  that  payments  of  mobilization
   expenses to contractors  be  supported  with  complete  documentation  as
   prescribed to prove the validity, legality and propriety thereof.

   As regards the mobilization expenses amounting to  P584,734.03  in  2018,
   the corresponding letter- requests and the irrevocable letters of  credit
   or  bank  guarantees  from/issued  by  the  latter   be   submitted   for
   verification. Should the documents be  not  submitted  or  found  not  in
   order, the disbursements may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management install and maintain a system of  internal
   control for the procurement, recording and  monitoring  of  Supplies  and
   Property, Plant and Equipment, as follows:

      • The GSO should be responsible in  accepting  procured  goods  after
        inspection. Thus, he should not be designated  as  Inspector  or  a
        canvasser.;

      • The procured items be received, accepted and recorded in the  Stock
        or Property Cards by the Supplies/Property Officer. Stock Cards and
        Property  Cards  be  maintained  for  each  type  of  Supplies/PPE,
        respectively.;

      • The Supply/Property Officer issues supplies/small PPE items to  the
        requesting office or end-users. The issuances should  be  supported
        with Requisition and Issue Slips. The issuance of small  items  not
        to be considered as PPE should be covered with Inventory  Custodian
        Slips (ICS).;

      • The Accounting Office should also maintain complete Supplies Ledger
        Cards and PPE Ledger Cards for supplies and PPE, respectively.; and

      • The Supply/Property Office and Accounting  Office  reconcile  their
        respective records regularly.

We recommended  that  Management  ensure  that  programs,  projects,  and
   activities (PPAs)  for  Solid  Waste  Management  are  feasible  and  are
   implemented as programmed to realize/serve the intended purposes.

We recommended that Management revisit  its  staffing  pattern.  In  this
   regard, the LGU may consider filling in  some  of  its  vacant  plantilla
   positions and reduce the number of job order  workers,  thus,  facilitate
   monitoring of workers and establish more accountability at work.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management consider the charging of
   a minimal mark-up or profit to  shoulder  the  expenses  and/or  possible
   losses in the handling and safekeeping of the goods prior to sale.  Also,
   Management establish  an  appropriate  stock  level  of  rice  and  other
   agricultural supplies to avoid under/over stocking.

We recommended that Management maintain and use a current account instead of a savings account for all its deposits/receipts and withdrawals/disbursements as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009, thus, ensure the fair presentation of the Cash in Bank account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management reclassified the Cash in Bank- LCSA to Cash in Bank-LCCA

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the accountable officials concerned be required to liquidate their outstanding cash advances/settle their respective accountabilities, thus, reflecting the fair balances of the Government Equity and the related asset and/or expense accounts.
observations.reason: There were still unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2018. Hence, reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, thus, fairly present the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: The reciprocal accounts were not yet reconciled as of December 31, 2018. It is noteworthy, though, that the unreconciled balance was reduced. Hence, reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management exert extra efforts to collect the receivables from the accountable persons/entities concerned. Should collection be no longer possible, the Municipal Mayor may request for authority to write-off the long outstanding receivable accounts from the Commission on Audit following the specific guidelines and procedures as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005, thus, fairly present the affected accounts in the books of the LGU.
observations.reason: There were still Other Receivables which remained outstanding as of December 31, 2018 including, among others, the account of the former Municipal Treasurer amounting to P391,903.11 which has been outstanding for years and which collection may no longer be possible. Hence, reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that all financial assistance granted to other LGUs and to NGAs for specified purposes be taken up in the books of accounts as receivables from the recipients to establish accountability and demand for liquidation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant determine the details or nature of the variance amounting to P103,128.88 and, thereafter, take up the necessary adjustments to present fairly the account-Due to NGAs in the books of the LGU.
observations.reason: The balance of the account Due to NGAs as of December 31, 2018 did not still tally with the balance per supporting schedule. The discrepancy amounted to P2,007,800.94. It is noteworthy, though, that the Municipal Accountant is exerting effort to determine the details of the discrepancy amounting to P103,128.88.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management return/refund the unutilized balances of the fund transfers to the source National Government Agencies to fully liquidate the same, thus, reduce the liability of the LGU.
observations.reason: Based on the Schedule of Fund Transfers from NGAs as of December 31, 2018, there were unutilized balances which were not yet refunded or returned to the source agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts, as follows: Reclassify the cost of the rice and agricultural supplies of P1,064,768.12 from Other Payables to Merchandise Inventory.; and Restate the description of the account/transaction in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management ensure that all financial assistance granted to other LGUs and to NGAs like the PNP be supported with complete documentation to prove the validity, legality and propriety thereof.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the employees concerned to submit the lacking documents, otherwise, the amount of P30,678.00 be refunded.
observations.reason: Some disbursements for travel expenses in 2018 were still not in accord with the prescribed rates.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that all procurements for repairs and maintenance are supported with complete documentation to prove the validity, legality and propriety of the transactions. In this regard, the lacking documents for the repairs and maintenance amounting to P998,415.50 be submitted for verification. Should the documents be not submitted or be found not in order, the disbursements may be suspended or disallowed in audit.
observations.reason: Some disbursements for repairs and maintenance in 2018 were still not supported with complete documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that payments of RATA are supported with complete documentation as prescribed. In this regard, the required lacking documents for the payments of RATA for January to March 2017 amounting to P744,763.00 be submitted for verification.

We recommended that Management ensure that payments of terminal leave benefits (TLB) be supported with complete documentation as prescribed to prove the validity and accuracy of the disbursements. Correspondingly, the lacking documents and the necessary corrective documents for the deficiencies noted on the payments of the TLB totaling P270,818.98 be submitted to avoid the possible suspension or disallowance thereof.

We recommended that Management submit the lacking documents for the purchase of relief goods amounting to P279,849.60, including the justification for the variance of P31,519.60, for verification. Should the documents be not submitted or found not in order, the disbursements may be suspended or disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management ensure that all procurements of the LGU charged against the Special Education Fund (SEF) be supported with complete documentation as prescribed. In this regard, the lacking and necessary corrective documents for the procurement of printing and binding of books amounting to P115,088.00 be submitted for verification. Should the documents be not submitted or found not in order, the disbursement may be suspended of disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management ensure that all procurements of the LGU be supported with complete documentation to prove the validity, legality and propriety of the transactions. In this regard, the lacking and/or necessary corrective documents for the procured construction materials amounting to P77,990.00 be submitted for verification. Should the documents be not submitted or found not in order, the disbursement may be suspended or disallowed in audit.
observations.reason: Some procurements in 2018 were still not supported with complete documentation. Audit observations on incomplete documentation are included in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that all contracts submitted to the Office of the Auditor be supported with complete documents as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate the systematic and effective review and evaluation thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure timely submission of accounts. Accordingly, she should cause the immediate submission of the unsubmitted accounts of the LGU for CY 2017 to facilitate the audit of the transactions as well as to avoid the possible suspension or disallowance of the same and the imposition of administrative disciplinary sanctions.
observations.reason: The submission of accounts in 2018 improved compared to the previous year. However, some accounts were still not submitted to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management appropriate 1% or even more than 1%, if necessary, of the LGU’s Internal Revenue Allotment for the strengthening and implementation of the programs, projects and activities of the Local Council for the Protection of Children.
We recommended that Management strictly observe the minimum monthly net take home pay of the officials and employees of the LGU as required under Section 47 of the General Provisions of the GAA.
observations.reason: There were still net pays in 2018 below the minimum amount prescribed under the GAA, i.e. P5,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management monitor and ensure the implementation and completion of all the LGU’s programs, projects and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund to immediately serve the intended purpose or use thereof to the constituents.
observations.reason: Some PPAs under the 20% Development Fund were not implemented as of December 31, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that the GAD plan and budget be implemented/used to fully realize the intended purpose/s of the identified programs, projects and activities.
observations.reason: Some PPAs for Gender and Development (GAD) were not implemented as of December 31, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that the LDRRM plan and budget be implemented/used to fully realize the intended purpose/s of the identified programs, projects and activities.
observations.reason: Some PPAs for LDRRM were not implemented as of December 31, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: The Municipal Accountant draw a Journal Entry Voucher to debit back the total amount to Cash in Bank and a credit to Accounts Payable while remain unclaimed by the payee. To issue a check replacement as soon as the stale check will be presented by the payee in consonance with Section 59 paragraph 3 of the afore -cited regulation.
observations.reason: There are still long outstanding/stale checks as of December 31, 2018 which are for replacement.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the following: That the Municipal Mayor causes the immediate submission of the required documents to justify the legality/validity of the above-mentioned disbursement to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit. That the Municipal Accounting Office conducts a thorough pre- audit on the completeness of supporting documents before approval of payment to avoid possible wastage of LGU fund.

We recommended that Management maintain and use a current account instead
of   a   savings   account   for   all    its    deposits/receipts    and
withdrawals/disbursements as prescribed under COA Circular No.  2015-009,
thus, ensure the fair presentation of the Cash in  Bank  account  in  the
financial statements.

We recommended that the accountable officials concerned  be  required  to
liquidate  their  outstanding  cash  advances/settle   their   respective
accountabilities, thus, reflecting the fair balances  of  the  Government
Equity and the related asset and/or expense accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal  Accountant  reconcile  the  reciprocal
accounts Due from Other Funds  and  Due  to  Other  Funds,  thus,  fairly
present the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended  that  Management  exert  extra  efforts  to  collect  the
receivables  from  the  accountable  persons/entities  concerned.  Should
collection be no longer possible, the Municipal  Mayor  may  request  for
authority to write-off the long outstanding receivable accounts from  the
Commission on Audit following the specific guidelines and  procedures  as
prescribed under COA Circular No.  2016-005,  thus, fairly present the
affected accounts in the books of the LGU.

We recommended that  Management  ensure  that  all  financial  assistance
granted to other LGUs and to NGAs for specified purposes be taken  up  in
the books of accounts as receivables from  the  recipients  to  establish
accountability and demand for liquidation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant  determine  the  details  or
nature of the variance amounting to P103,128.88 and, thereafter, take  up
the necessary adjustments to present fairly the account-Due  to  NGAs  in
the books of the LGU.

We recommended that Management return/refund the unutilized  balances  of
the fund transfers to the source National Government  Agencies  to  fully
liquidate the same, thus, reduce the liability of the LGU.

We recommended that  the  Municipal  Accountant  take  up  the  necessary
adjustments in the books of accounts, as follows:

 •  Reclassify  the  cost  of  the  rice  and  agricultural  supplies  of
   P1,064,768.12 from Other Payables to Merchandise Inventory.; and

 • Restate the description of the account/transaction  in  the  Notes  to
   Financial Statements.

We recommended the
